mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly payroll gains signal sustained economic expansion, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC of PAYEMS-based signal on 20-day returns below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 3%, tightening financial conditions compress equity valuations and increase recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or hit rate > 55% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real interest rates, indicated by inflation exceeding the policy rate, reduce the discount rate on equities and encourage risk-taking, boosting S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show mean <= 0 when real rates are negative, or Rank IC of joint condition < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth above 5% YoY in a late-cycle expansion signals overheating and tightening risk, causing a negative reaction in S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns in triggered instances are positive more than 50% of the time, or mean return > 0 with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth reflects robust manufacturing activity and rising corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positively correlated with INDPRO growth (Pearson r < 0.1) or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,500 thousand units signals robust construction demand and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as housing investment multipliers propagate.
failure_condition: 60-day returns in triggered periods are not significantly positive (one-sided Wilcoxon test p > 0.05) or hit rate for positive returns < 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Very low unemployment combined with high payroll levels indicates a tight labor market that pushes up wages and inflation expectations, prompting Fed tightening and lowering equity prices over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in triggered periods are not significantly negative (one-sample t-test μ ≥ 0) or Fama-MacBeth regression coefficient on joint condition is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth slows below 2% and the fed funds rate is low, the economy is in a late-cycle easy money trap where diminishing returns on stimulus and rising risk premia cause negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during triggered regime have mean >= 0, or the joint signal's Rank IC fails below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling period.
